title: Listening - Part 3: Joining a Sports Centre
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they joined a particular sports centre. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the centre. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they joined a particular sports centre. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for choosing the centre. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about joining a sports centre.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I’m a nurse and my shifts change from week to week. I tried a gym near home before, but it closed just when I was finishing late shifts, and I hardly used my membership. [19]The sports centre I joined is open from very early in the morning until nearly midnight, so whatever hours I'm working, I can normally find time to exercise.[/19] The equipment isn't particularly modern, but being able to go regularly matters far more to me than impressive machines.||Speaker 2 Man: I used to swim regularly at school and missed it after moving away for university. Several gyms were within walking distance of my flat, but they only had exercise machines and classes. [20]I chose the sports centre on the other side of town because it has a full-sized swimming pool where I can train properly instead of giving up the activity I most enjoy.[/20] Getting there by bus takes longer, yet I never mind that once I'm in the water.||Speaker 3 Woman: After retiring, I realised I could easily go through an entire day without having a proper conversation with anyone. My doctor suggested more exercise, but walking alone didn't solve that problem. [21]At my local sports centre there are relaxed badminton sessions and a café where members chat afterwards, so joining it seemed a good way to be active while getting to know people.[/21] I now look forward to Tuesdays and Thursdays, not because I'm a brilliant player, but because I enjoy the company.||Speaker 4 Man: I wanted to start lifting weights, but videos online gave completely different advice, and I was worried about injuring my back by practising incorrectly. One centre near me offered a cheaper membership. [22]I chose the slightly more expensive one because an instructor assesses new members, shows them how to use equipment safely and prepares a training plan for their goals.[/22] Having somebody knowledgeable to ask has made me much more confident, particularly when increasing the weights I use.||Speaker 5 Woman: I’ve always found exercise machines boring, and if I have to pay extra for each fitness session, I know I’ll decide not to attend. I compared several local centres before choosing one. [23]My membership includes yoga, dance and cycling classes without additional charges, so I can try different organised sessions throughout the week and stay motivated.[/23] I've already discovered that I like dance far more than I expected. Paying once also means I don't have to make a decision every time a class interests me.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I’m a nurse and my shifts change from week to week. I tried a gym near home before, but it closed just when I was finishing late shifts, and I hardly used my membership. The sports centre I joined is open from very early in the morning until nearly midnight, so whatever hours I'm working, I can normally find time to exercise. The equipment isn't particularly modern, but being able to go regularly matters far more to me than impressive machines.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I used to swim regularly at school and missed it after moving away for university. Several gyms were within walking distance of my flat, but they only had exercise machines and classes. I chose the sports centre on the other side of town because it has a full-sized swimming pool where I can train properly instead of giving up the activity I most enjoy. Getting there by bus takes longer, yet I never mind that once I'm in the water.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: After retiring, I realised I could easily go through an entire day without having a proper conversation with anyone. My doctor suggested more exercise, but walking alone didn't solve that problem. At my local sports centre there are relaxed badminton sessions and a café where members chat afterwards, so joining it seemed a good way to be active while getting to know people. I now look forward to Tuesdays and Thursdays, not because I'm a brilliant player, but because I enjoy the company.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I wanted to start lifting weights, but videos online gave completely different advice, and I was worried about injuring my back by practising incorrectly. One centre near me offered a cheaper membership. I chose the slightly more expensive one because an instructor assesses new members, shows them how to use equipment safely and prepares a training plan for their goals. Having somebody knowledgeable to ask has made me much more confident, particularly when increasing the weights I use.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I’ve always found exercise machines boring, and if I have to pay extra for each fitness session, I know I’ll decide not to attend. I compared several local centres before choosing one. My membership includes yoga, dance and cycling classes without additional charges, so I can try different organised sessions throughout the week and stay motivated. I've already discovered that I like dance far more than I expected. Paying once also means I don't have to make a decision every time a class interests me.

options.text: It includes group activities in the price.
It has facilities for a particular sport.
It is close to their workplace.
It gives them opportunities to meet people.
Its membership is cheaper than elsewhere.
It provides activities for their children.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker chose a centre whose long opening hours suit changing work shifts (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The availability of a suitable swimming pool determined the speaker's choice (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker joined because the centre provides social contact alongside exercise (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The centre was selected because qualified staff offer guidance and safe training plans (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker values having a choice of group classes included in the membership cost (evidence [23]).
